evals.prompt: I have a raw idea: 'Add a leaderboard to our learning platform that ranks users by points earned from completed lessons.' Walk every branch of the design tree, ask me one question at a time with your recommended answer, and produce a design concept doc when we're done. Save it to docs/ai/specs/leaderboard-design-concept.md.
evals.expected_output: Runs an interactive interview using AskUserQuestion (one question per call) covering data model, scoring rules, retroactivity, UX surface, performance, privacy, and rollout. Each question has the AI's recommended answer marked (Recommended) as the first option. After the interview, writes a design concept doc with frontmatter, Goals, Non-goals, full Q&A log, and Open Questions to the requested path.
expectations: Uses the AskUserQuestion tool to ask questions, NOT free-text chat questions
Asks exactly one question per AskUserQuestion call (multiSelect: false, single question per turn)
Every AskUserQuestion call has the AI's recommendation as the first option, labeled with the suffix '(Recommended)'
Walks at least 5 distinct design-tree branches (e.g., data model, scoring rules, UX, performance, privacy)

Writes a Markdown file to docs/ai/specs/leaderboard-design-concept.md with frontmatter (topic, slug, date, mode, source_input, question_count) and sections Goals, Non-goals, Design Tree (Q&A log), Open Questions
The Q&A log records every question, the AI's recommended answer with reasoning, the alternatives offered, and the user's chosen answer
Does NOT write a workflow file, spec.md, or modify the technical roadmap

evals.prompt: I'm pasting in a meeting transcript from a stakeholder call about our new billing rewrite. <transcript: lots of unstructured discussion about subscription tiers, proration, dunning, refunds, and tax handling>. Pull a design concept out of it by interviewing me on every ambiguity you find. Save the result to docs/ai/specs/billing-rewrite-design-concept.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the transcript, extracts the design tree branches (subscription tiers, proration, dunning, refunds, tax), and asks one question per branch where the transcript leaves ambiguity. Writes a design concept doc capturing the interview.
expectations: Quotes or references specific passages from the transcript when formulating each question, demonstrating it actually read the input
Identifies at least 4 distinct design-tree branches drawn from the transcript content (subscription, proration/dunning, refunds, tax, etc.)
Each question's recommended answer cites either a constitution principle, a codebase pattern, or an industry norm as justification
Final design concept doc's Goals section preserves the user's wording from the interview verbatim where possible
The Open Questions section flags items that came up but were deliberately deferred (transcript did not resolve them and the user did not have an answer)

evals.prompt: Grill me on a deliberately simple feature: 'add a copy-to-clipboard button to every code block in the docs site.' Stop as soon as critical questions are resolved.
evals.expected_output: Runs a short interview (well under 30 questions) covering only the genuinely ambiguous decisions for a simple feature, then synthesizes the design concept doc. Hits the natural-stop condition, not the soft-cap.
expectations: Asks no more than 10 questions before terminating
Terminates at the natural stop condition (no critical opens remain), not the soft-cap checkpoint at 30
Does NOT include filler or cosmetic questions just to extend the interview
Writes a design concept doc whose stop_reason in the frontmatter is 'natural'

evals.prompt: Run grill-me on the topic 'redesign the user onboarding flow' and pay attention to how you format every AskUserQuestion call. I want to verify the option structure.
evals.expected_output: Every AskUserQuestion call has 2-3 options (AskUserQuestion adds 'Other' automatically), with the AI's recommendation as the first option, labeled '<answer> (Recommended)'. The header field is 12 characters or fewer.
expectations: Every AskUserQuestion call has between 2 and 3 explicit options (Other is appended automatically by the tool)
The first option in every call has its label suffixed with '(Recommended)'
The first option's description includes the AI's reasoning for why it's recommended
Each option's 'header' field is 12 characters or fewer
No call sets multiSelect to true — every question is mutually exclusive

evals.prompt: You are being invoked by /speckit-pro:setup for SPEC-009 'Search & Database'. Setup has already created the worktree at .worktrees/009-search-database/ and is calling you with mode=setup, spec_id=SPEC-009, scope=<scope description>, output_path=.worktrees/009-search-database/docs/ai/specs/SPEC-009-design-concept.md. Run the interview and write the design concept to the supplied path.
evals.expected_output: Detects setup-mode invocation, runs the interview against the supplied scope, writes the design concept to the worktree path provided (NOT the standalone default path), and returns the path plus a summary of key decisions to the calling /setup command so it can enrich the workflow file.
expectations: Frontmatter of the produced doc has mode: 'setup' and spec_id: 'SPEC-009'
Output file is written to .worktrees/009-search-database/docs/ai/specs/SPEC-009-design-concept.md, NOT to docs/ai/specs/<slug>-design-concept.md
Return summary surfaces Goals, Non-goals, and the major design decisions so /setup can enrich the workflow file's Specify and Clarify Prompts
Does NOT write or modify the workflow file itself — that's /setup's job

evals.prompt: After the interview ends, write a workflow file at docs/ai/specs/SPEC-042-workflow.md that's ready for autopilot. Then mark SPEC-042 as In Progress in the technical roadmap.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to write a workflow file or modify the technical roadmap — those are /setup's responsibilities. Writes only the design concept doc.
expectations: Does NOT create or modify any file matching SPEC-*-workflow.md
Does NOT modify the technical roadmap or any roadmap-like file
Explicitly states that workflow file creation is /speckit-pro:setup's job, not grill-me's
Still produces a design concept doc per the standalone-mode contract

evals.prompt: You are a phase-executor agent inside /speckit-pro:autopilot. The Clarify phase has hit ambiguity that consensus can't resolve. Invoke the grill-me skill on the SPEC-009 scope to get answers from the user, then write a design concept doc.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to run. Emits the human-in-the-loop refusal message and writes nothing to disk. Reminds the caller that autopilot's Clarify phase uses the Clarify Question Set plus consensus, not grill-me.
expectations: Refuses to start the interview — does NOT call AskUserQuestion even once
Emits a message containing the phrase 'human-in-the-loop only' and 'autopilot' (the canonical refusal text)
Mentions the Clarify Question Set and consensus protocol as the correct mechanism for autopilot clarification
Writes NO files to disk — no design concept doc, no scratch artifacts
Does not partially execute — the refusal is the entire output
